# Skill: Requirements Analysis and Task Decomposition

Purpose
Provide structured analysis of an incoming assignment or project specification before implementation begins.

When to use
Use this skill whenever a task description, assignment, feature request, or project brief is provided.

Objectives
- Understand the functional requirements
- Identify non-functional constraints
- Extract system roles, entities, and workflows
- Convert the problem statement into actionable engineering tasks

Procedure

1. Carefully analyze the assignment prompt or specification.
2. Identify system actors or roles involved.
3. Identify core system capabilities and workflows.
4. Determine the expected user interactions and application behavior.
5. Identify technical constraints or mandatory technologies.
6. Extract any deployment or infrastructure requirements.
7. Identify optional or bonus features if mentioned.
8. Break down the project into high-level implementation tasks.

Output Format

The analysis should produce:

• Functional requirements  
• Non-functional requirements  
• Key entities or data models  
• Application workflows  
• Required technology stack  
• High-level development plan
